name: Mixed Vegetable Cutlets
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Green peas, frozen or fresh
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the cauliflower, potatoes, fresh peas and green beans in the bottom of a pot. Cover with water. Put carrots, beets, and celery in a steaming basket, cover and place over the pot.
2. Cook until vegetables are tender-crisp. Drain, reserving some water.
3. Add frozen peas (defrosted first) to the steamed vegetables.
4. In a blender, blend with running motor, add chilies. Add chickpeas, nuts, and seeds; process until peas are mashed. Some vegetable water may help. Add cauliflower, potatoes, fresh peas, if using, and beans. Pulse on and off until coarsely mashed.
5. Transfer to mixing bowl, add steamed vegetables, salt, pepper, turmeric, and garam masala. Mix well.
6. Divide into portions.
7. Line baking sheet with waxed paper. Flatten portion into an almond-shaped cutlet, 1/2 inch thick. Place on sheet. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
8. Whisk soy milk, cornstarch, and flour in a flat bowl until smooth. Dip each cutlet into batter, roll in poppy seeds briefly to air dry.
9. Heat ghee to depth of 3/4 inch without crowding. Fry cutlets in batches for 2 minutes per side. Drain and serve.
